rulings.Case Title: INVESTEK PROPERTIES VS ALTA MESA WIND
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, November 1, 2013, Line 1, DEFENDANT MARK JONES' DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Sustained with 20 days leave to amend. Plaintiffs' request for judicial notice is granted. As to the first cause of action, the Third Amended Complaint ("TAC") does not meet the pleading standard for alter ego liability set forth in Leek v. Cooper. ((2011) 194 Cal.App.4th 399.) The TAC does not state specific facts showing a unity of interest or that an inequitable result would result if alter ego liability was not imposed. As to the second cause of action, the TAC does not allege facts sufficient to establish that Defendant, as an individual, owed a fiduciary duty to Plaintiffs. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: ELBA LUCERO et al VS. DALE T. BOUTIETTE et al
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, November 1, 2013, Line 3, DEFENDANTs DALE BOUTIETTE, BOUTIETTE + VON HERMANN, LLP and BVH LAW GROUP, LLP's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. The renewed motion for summary judgment is denied. Even considering the "new" evidence, there remains a triable issue of material fact regarding (1) when plaintiff discovered defendant Boutiette's alleged malpractice; and (2) when defendant Boutiette's representation of plaintiff ended. (Declaration of Henry Lucero dated 11/30/12, pars. 3, 4, 6 and 7; Declaration of Henry Lucero dated 10/18/13, pars. 2 and 3; Declaration of Theodore Bayer dated October 18, 2013, par. 3; Ex. B to Declaration of Robert Cross (Deposition of Henry Lucero, 424:5-425:6, 427:25-428:23)). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: JASON WONG VS. BARBARA JIANG et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, November 1, 2013, Line 2: Defendant BARBARA JIANG'S Motion To Compel Responses Pro Tem Judge Peter Van Zandt, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendants Jiang and Nursing Care Pharmacies, Inc.¿s Motion to Compel Responses to Interrogatories is GRANTED. Plaintiff Jason Wong is to provide original, verified and complete responses, without objection to: 1. Jiang¿s form interrogatories, set one and two 2. Jiang¿s special interrogatories, set two 3. Nursing Care Pharmacies form interrogatories, set two 4. Nursing Care Pharmacies special interrogatories, set one Plaintiff Jason Wong must serve these responses within 15 days of service of Notice of Entry of the Order on this motion. Sanctions in the amount of $1,500 in a check made payable to Luscutoff, Lendormy & Associates must accompany the service of the responses. Any party who contests this tentative ruling must send an email to peter.vanzandt@leclairryan.com or a fax to 415.391.8766 with a copy to all other parties by 4pm one day prior to the hearing stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem Judge hearing the motion and the Pro Tem Judge will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)
rulings.Judge: Judge Pro Tem: Peter Van Zandt

If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. rulings.Case Title: IRIS CABRERA VS. JOEL CAMPOS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn For U-Visa Certification
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, November 1, 2013, Line 18, PLAINTIFF IRIS CABRERA's Motion For U-Visa Certification. This motion is off calendar. CCP § 1005(b) requires that moving papers be filed at least 16 court days before the hearing. The moving papers were filed on October 10, 2013, which is only 15 court days before November 1, 2013. Additionally, no proof of service was provided. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: 851 CALIFORNIA STREET, LLC VS. NELSON BROTHERS PROFESSIONAL REAL ESTATE, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel 1) Compliance With Discovery Responses; 2) Compliance With Deposition Notice; And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, November 1, 2013, Line 7: Plaintiff 851 CALIFORNIA STREET, LLC'S Motion To Compel 1) Compliance With Discovery Responses; 2) Compliance With Deposition Notice; And For Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Peter VanZandt, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff 851 California, LLC¿s Motion to Compel Defendant Nelson Brothers Professional Real Estate, LLC¿s Compliance with Agreement to Produce Documents and Compliance with Deposition Notice is GRANTED. Local counsel for the Parties are ORDERED to appear at 8:30 am in the hallway in front of Department 301 or at a place and time of their choosing prior to that to meet and confer on dates for production of the documents and date for the deposition. Following the meet and confer, counsel for the parties will advise the Court of the agreed upon dates and an Order will follow. If the parties fail to agree in advance of the hearing, the matter will be heard. The request for sanctions is DENIED in this tentative ruling but will be revisited as to counsel for both parties if the hearing goes forward. Any party who contests this tentative ruling must send an email to peter.vanzandt@leclairryan.com or a fax to 415.391.8766 with a copy to all other parties by 4pm one day prior to the hearing stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem Judge hearing the motion and the Pro Tem Judge will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: KELKRIS ASSOCIATES, INC. VS. RYE R. KONG et al
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, November 1, 2013, Line 19, DEFENDANT HEIDI KONG's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. The Court grants defendant's request for judicial notice. The demurrer is sustained without leave to amend. The pre and post-petition assessments were discharged under 11 USC 1328(a). See Matter of Mattera 203 B.R. 565, 572 (Bankr.D.N.J.1997). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: MEDHAT MAHGOUB VS. SERGIO AUGUSTO ROMAN et al
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, November 1, 2013, Line 20, DEFENDANT SERGIO ROMAN's MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. The Motion to Strike is granted with 10 days leave to amend for the plaintiff to plead, if he can do so in good faith, allegations supporting a punitive damages claim. Plaintiff has failed to plead the requisite malice under Civ. Code, § 3294. (See Taylor v. Superior Court (1979) 24 Cal.3d 890.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)
